titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Hajo Holborn Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Ruth Gay and staff of Manuscripts and Archives

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid created in accordance with manuscripts and archives' processing manual.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday May 24, 2019 at 07:16
finding aid revisiion description not supplied

did.abstract: The papers consist chiefly of notes, manuscripts, documents, minutes of parliamentary committees, pamphlets and other materials collected by Hajo Holborn in connection with research on the drafting of the Weimar constitution. The pamphlets include works on related subjects, such as the German revolution of 1918, the Workers' and Soldiers' Councils, constitutional history and federalism in Germany, territorial questions concerning Silesia and the Saar, and materials on German political parties, including minutes of meetings, leaflets and printed matter from the Deutsche Demokratische Partei (DDP). Some of the DDP material is particularly addressed to women. Also in the papers are essays and reviews by Holborn, teaching materials from his tenure at the Hochschule für Politik in Berlin and Yale University. There is only a small amount of correspondence, largely on professional matters. Important correspondents are Friedrich Meinecke and Alfred Vagts, who also contributed a memoir on Holborn.

langmaterial.language: The materials are in English and German.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Hajo Holborn Papers (MS 579). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from the Yale University History Department, 1969; gift of Hanna H. Gray, 2012.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: Series II, Boxes 4-24 (printed pamphlets, books, and documents concerning the constitution of the Weimar Republic) are available on microfilm (19,486 frames on 19 reels, 35mm.) from Scholarly Resources, Inc., Wilmington, Delaware.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in two series: I. Office Files. II. Subject Files, and two additions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers of Hajo Holborn came to Manuscripts and Archives from the History Department of Yale University in September 1969, several months after Holborn's death in June of that year. They are made up of the contents of his office files and contain few letters and personal documents. (A preliminary inventory was prepared by Professor Hans Gatzke of the Yale University History Department in 1971 and was the only guide to the papers until this register was completed in 1981.) Other records have unfortunately disappeared. Although the papers do not cover the full range of Holborn's interests, they offer a remarkably coherent reflection of his activities ca. 1920-1935.

The papers have been divided into two series: OFFICE FILES and SUBJECT FILES. The OFFICE FILES contain surviving correspondence, negotiations with various universities and publishers, and drafts and reprints of several essays and reviews, offprints sent him by colleagues, and memorabilia. Through the kindness of Alfred Vagts, we have electrostatic copies of twelve letters from Holborn to Vagts concerning his emigration to the United States and his later academic career (1934-1941). Together with these is a brief memoir on Holborn by Vagts, probably written ca. 1976.

The SUBJECT FILES consist very largely of printed matter arranged topically. The major portion of this material was gathered in connection with a commission received by Holborn in 1929 from the Historische Reichskommission to write a history of the constitution of the Weimar Republic. (See Box 1, folder 4.) The materials on this topic which constitute a major part of the papers are made up of Holborn's own handwritten and typed notes, pamphlets, periodicals, handbills, typescript copies of documents, and other papers. Most vivid in charting the writing of the constitution are the large number of working papers issued by the Constitutional Assembly (Die verfassunggebende deutsche Nationalversammlung) which were originally in the possession of Dr. Marie Baum, a member of the Assembly and of the Deutsche Demokratische Partei (DDP). A letter from Hugo Preuss (then Staatssekretär des Innern) transmitting the first official draft (1919 Jan 7) of the constitution and a memorandum of explanation contains extensive comment on the draft in an unidentified hand. (Box 12, folder 95.) Many other stages of the constitution are included in the papers, as are counter drafts by interest groups and petitions from industry, labor organizations, local communitites, the elderly, small landholders, etc. seeking to influence the decisions of the Assembly. Rare records on the origins of the DDP, also from Dr. Baum, contain handbills and election pamphlets dealing with the role of women within the party, pamphlets issued by the party discussing issues of the day, and proceedings of some of its meetings in 1919. Also of importance are the pamphlets on the Workers' and Soldiers' Councils of 1918-1919, including a rare copy of Allgemeiner Kongress der Arbeiter und Soldatenräte Deutschlands vom 16.-21. Dec. 1918. Stenographische Berichte , as well as contemporary reports by and about councils in Bavaria, Berlin, and Hamburg.

The bulk of the series is made up of pamphlets and books issued between 1919 and 1929. Many were published by small presses in small editions and are now difficult to find. Of those that survived, many were printed on cheap paper and are in poor condition. There are also some 19th century pamphlets on early attempts at a constitution for Germany and discussions of the constitutional question. A bibliography, probably drawn up by Holborn in connection with this project (Box 19, folder 170), has been annotated by Manuscripts and Archives staff to indicate which pamphlets are in the collection and to note their location. Taken together, the combination of printed matter, documents, and clippings provides a rich resource for the study of the beginnings of the German Democratic Party, the Revolution of 1918-1919, the Workers' and Soldiers' Councils, as well as the constitutional foundation of the Weimar Republic.

Nine books which were seriously damaged in a flood at Manuscripts and Archives in 1980 had to be discarded. These are listed at the end of the register. Fortunately duplicate copies of these books are in the stacks of Sterling Memorial Library and are available to the reader.

In order to preserve the printed matter in the collection and also make it more accessible, the books and pamphlets have been filmed and are available on MICROFILM HM 118. An author and subject guide provides access to the reels.

For related material in Manuscripts and Archives, see the German Pamphlet Collection for propaganda on the Ruhr and Silesia and the last days of the Weimar Republic. For material on the Versailles Treaty, reparations, and the post-World War I period, see the Frank L. Polk Papers Mss 656 and the Edward M. House Papers Mss 466.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Hajo Holborn was educated in Germany. From 1926-1931 he was a professor of history at the University of Heidelberg. Holborn was the Carnegie Professor of History and International Relations at the School of Politics in Berlin from 1931-1934. He was a professor of history at Yale University from 1934-1969.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: BOOKS DAMAGED IN 1980 AND DISCARDED (For duplicates, see Orbis)

list.item: Anschütz, Gerhard. Die Verfassung des Deutschen Reichs vom 11. August 1919: Ein Kommentar für Wissenschaft und Praxis . Vierte Bearbeitung, 14. Auflage. Stilkes Rechtsbibliothek Nr. 1. Berlin: Georg Stilke, 1933.
Buchner, Eberhard. Revolutionsdokumente: Im Zeichen der roten Fahne. Erster Band . Berlin: Deutsche Verlagsgesellschaft für Politik und Geschichte m.b.H., 1921.

Deutscher Geschichtskalendar: Sachlich geordnete Zusammenstellung der wichtigsten Vorgänge im In-und Ausland . Der europäische Krieg in aktenmässiger Darstellung. Ergänzungsband: Die deutsche Revolution. Erster Band, November 1918-February 1919. Zweiter Band, März bis Juni 1919. Ed. Friedrich Purlitz. Leipzig: Felix Meiner, 1919.
____. Ergänzungsband: Die deutsche Reichsverfassung vom 11. August 1919. Ed. Friedrich Purlitz. Leipzig: Felix Meiner, 1919.
French, Robert Dudley. The Memorial Quadrangle: A Book about Yale . New Haven, Ct.: Yale University Press, 1929.
Müller-Franken, Hermann. Die Novemberrevolution: Erinnerungen . Berlin: Der Bücherkries G.M.B.H., 1928.

Sozialdemokratische Partei Deutschlands. Protokoll über die Verhandlungen Parteitages abgehalten in Weimar vom 10.-15. Juni 1919, sowie Bericht über die 7. Frauenkonferenz abgehalten in Weimar am 15. und 16. Juni 1919 . Berlin: Buchhandlung Vorwärts Paul Singer G.M.B.H., 1919.
Volkmann, Erich Otto. Der Marxismus und das deutsche Heer im Weltkriege . Berlin: Reimar Hobbing, 1925.
____. Revolution über Deutschland . Oldenburg i.o.: Gerhard Stalling, 1930.
Anschütz, Gerhard: Die Verfassung des Deutschen Reichs vom 11. August 1919. Mit Einleitung und Erläuterungen . 2nd edition. Berlin: Georg Stilke, 1921. 7th edition, 1928.
Kraus, Herbert: Das Recht der Minderheiten: Materialien zur Einfährung in das Verständnis des modernen Minoritätenproblems . Stilke's Rechtbibliothek Nr. 57. Berlin: Georg Stilke, 1927.
Marx, Karl: Revolution und Kontre-Revolution in Deutschland . Translated by K. Kautsky. 6th edition. Stuttgart: I.H.W. Dietz, 1920.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Series II, Boxes 4-24, is available on microfilm. Patrons must use HM 118 instead of the originals.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright is retained by Hannah H. Gray for unpublished works authored or otherwise produced by Hajo Holborn. After her lifetime or until September 11, 2020, whichever comes first, copyright passes to Yale University whether such works are at Yale or physically located elsewhere in the world. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence is arranged by writer and chiefly concerns Holborn's research on the Weimar constitution. Of particular interest is a letter (1930 Feb 2) from Friedrich Meinecke. Also in this section is business correspondence with the German universities where Holborn taught. Electrostatic copies of twelve letters (1934-1941) from Holborn to Alfred Vagts, together with a memoir by Vagts on Holborn, were contributed by Alfred Vagts in 1977. The letters document his emigration to the United States and the beginnings of his career here.
Speeches contains the text of several speeches, ca. 1930.
Teaching is arranged by institution and contains teaching materials from the Deutsche Hochschule für Politik and Yale University.

Writings is arranged chronologically and contains manuscript and typescript drafts as well as printed versions of articles and reviews by Holborn. Also included is some correspondence with publishers.
Memorabilia contains a variety of miscellaneous material, including a carbon copy of the will of Holborn's father, Ludwig Holborn.

did.unittitle: Holborn, Hajo, to Alfred Vagts (included also is a memoir on Holborn by Vagts [1976])
TMs, "Die Entstehung der Weimarer Verfassung als nationalpolitisches Problem"
Ms, "Vorgeschichte der Weimarer Verfassung. Räteherrschaft u. Demokratie"
TMs, [On the present situation in Germany]
TMs, "Some remarks on the present state of the peace problem"
Ms, review of Emil Ludwig's Bismarck . 2 drafts
"Bismarck und Schuwalow im Jahre 1875," Historische Zeitschrift (offprint)
Re: Books on Joseph Maria von Radowitz. Correspondence, TMs of an introduction, review
Ulrich von Hutten. Correspondence, financial statement
Die Europäische Politik, Archiv für Politik u. Geschichte (offprint)
Bismarck's europäische Politik ... und die Mission Radowitz. Contract, financial statements
Europäische Geschichte im Zeitalter der Restauration und Revolution 1815-1848. Outline, bibliography, and fragments of text (TMs)
La formation de la Constitution de Weimar problème de politique extérieure. (offprint)
"Verfassung und Verwaltung der deutschen Republik," Historische Zeitschrift (offprint)
Gedanken über das Studium der Aussen politik (TMs)
"Die geschichtlichen Grundlagen der deutschen Verfassungspolitik und Reichsreform," Deutsche Juristen Zeitung (galley proofs)
Die Entstehungsgeschichte des Artikels 8 der Völkerbundsatzung. (offprint)
Ulrich von Hutten. Correspondence, proofs, 1 photograph, 7 negatives for illustration, review
"Humanism in its influence in the rise of nationalism during the 15th and 16th century." (TMs)
"Memorandum of race and nationality aspects of post-war reconstruction and peace planning" (TMs)
Max Weber, TMs (carbon) and research notes; from a folder labelled "Quellen für Max Weber"
"The Prussian and German School of Strategy" (TMs)
Holborn, Ludwig (father of Hajo). Will (typescript carbon)
Krieger, Leonard, and Fritz Stern. The Responsibility of Power; Historical Essays in Honor of Hajo Holborn . Bound galleys

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: SUBJECT FILES, which consist almost entirely of pamphlets and printed documents, are arranged alphabetically by subject. Certain important figures for whom we have material (either by or about) have also been listed directly under their names: e.g. Friedrich Meinecke, Friedrich Naumann, and Hugo Preuss. The large number of documents issued by the National Assembly in 1919 are grouped together under the official name of the assembly: Die verfassunggebende deutsche Nationalversammlung. Non-governmental pamphlets on the constitution—its history, legal aspects, controversy over it, etc.—are grouped under the heading "Weimar constitution." An index to the pamphlets is at the end of the register.

Since the books and pamphlets are in poor condition, they have been filmed both to preserve them and for the convenience of the user. The reader interested in using only the pamphlets should use the microfilm. See MICROFILM HM 118. There is a subject and author guide to the reels.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This series is available on microfilm. Patrons must use FILM HM 118 instead of the originals.

did.unittitle: Not all of the Holborn papers listed in this register have been filmed. The items omitted are Hajo Holborn's academic papers and printed matter and memoranda not related to the Weimar period.
Bülow, Bernhard Heinrich Martin Karl, Fürst von (1849-1929)
Printed broadsides and newspapers relating to the Weimar Republic
Baum, Marie (1874-, Representative of the DDP): miscellaneous papers
DDP fraction at the Nationalversammlung, Ms minutes of meetings
DDP fraction at the Nationalversammlung, position papers on nationalization and worker participation
Executive committee, minutes of meetings and letter to the Hansabund
Women's convention: agenda, proposals, position papers
Frankfurter Messzeitung: Herausgegeben Vom Messamt der Frankfurter Internationalen Messen (Deutscher Werkbund)
"The Problem of Disarmament at the Peace Conference of Versailles," [by George V. Wolf?] (TMs)
Froebel, Julius (1805-1893). "Projekt eine Aktionsgesellschaft zur Uebernahm der Süddeutsche Presse" (Ms)
Grzesinski, Albert. La tragi-comédie de la république Allemande…
Horkenbach, Cuno. Das Deutsche Reich von 1918 bis heute
Bertzen, Etta M. S. von, "The co-operation of the press in the organization of the peace" (TMs)
Documents published by the Auswärtiges Amt
Exchange of notes between Germany and the Allies
Text of speech of Lloyd George
Entwurf des allgemeinen Teils der künftigen Reichsverfassung; "Denkschrift zum'Verfassungs entwurf"
Solf, Wilhelm (1862-1936): correspondence (typescript copies); interview with Maximillian von Hagen (TMs)
Ms notes from the Preussischen Innen-Ministerium, by Holborn
Auswértiges Amt: Der Aufstand im oberschlesischen Abstimmungsgebiet
Report of the Reichsarbeitsminister Bauer re: labor conditions; with 1 v. of related documents
Archival documents and newspaper accounts (Ms and typescript copies)
Bibliography probably compiled by Holborn; printed bibliography
Minutes of meetings of Friedrich Ebert with the Staatenvertretern, Berlin
Research notes: Ms and typescript (Microfilm, 1 reel)
Allgemeiner Kongress der Arbeiter und Soldatenräte Deutschlands, 16-21 Dec, 1918. Stenographische Bericht
Der Zentralrat . Mitteilungsblatt des Zentralrates der deutschen sozialistischen Republik. 1. Jahrgang. Nr. 1-16 [periodical]
"Was will die Deutsche Democratische Partei Hamburg?" (3 copies)
Newspapers and reprints re the Weimar Constitution
Hand-drawn maps (2) of Germany and surrounding countries indicating borders, fortifications, obstacles
Message du Président Wilson lu au Congrès des États-Unis d'Amérique
Waldecker, Ludwig: Die Verfassung des Freistaates Preussen vom 30 November 1920, Mit Erläuterungen und Sachregister . Berlin: Georg Stilke
Breitenstein, Desiderius: Die Sozialistische Erziehungsbewegung: ihre geistigen Grundlagen und ihr Verhältnis zum Marxismus . Freiburg im Breisgau: Herder & Co.
Naphtale, Fritz, Hrsg. Wirtschafts Demokratie. Ihr Wesen, Weg und Ziel. 4 , unverönderte Auglage. Berlin: Verlagsgesellschaft des Allgemeinen Deutschen Gewerkschaftsbundes GmbH.
Schwarz, Salomon. Handbuch der Gewerkschaftskongresse. (Kongresse des Allgemeinen Deutschen Gewerkschaftsbundes) . Berlin: Verlagsgesellschaft des Allgemeinen Deutschen Gewerkschaftsbundes
Sturmfels, Wilhelm. Die Bedeutung des Gewerkschaften für die Staatsbildung . Karlsruhe, Verlag G. Braun: 1928 (Wissen und Wirken. Gewerkschaften und Staat).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist primarily of correspondence to and from Hajo Holborn. Correspondents of note include Theodore Heuss, who served as the first President of the Federal Republic of Germany following the Second World War from 1949 to 1959; German historian Friedrich Meinecke; and pioneering electronic music composer, Werner Kaegi. Also included are writings by Holborn; a newspaper clipping; a copy of remarks delivered at a meeting of the Yale history department on the occasion of Holborn's death; and an essay about Holborn written by Dietrich Gerhard.

did.unittitle: Essay on Holborn by Dietrich Gerhard
Remarks at meeting of the History Department on Holborn's death
